methodology.auth: Session cookie obtained via /api/auth/login with dev@ship.local credentials

test_environment.api_env_vars: E2E_TEST=1 (raises rate limit from 1000 to 10000 req/min in dev)
test_environment.note_on_seed_volume: PDF requirement is 500+ documents; Ship's seed produced 257. Baseline still meaningful for relative before/after comparison since the same 257-doc dataset will be used for the after-improvement runs.

methodology_caveats.rate_limiting_interference: Ship's API has a rate limit configured at api/src/app.ts:81 (1000 req/min in dev, 10000 in test mode, 100 in production). Even at E2E_TEST=1 (10000/min), autocannon's max-throughput mode exceeds this for small-payload endpoints (dashboard, programs, weeks return small responses, so autocannon sends 5000-7000 req/s = 300000-420000 req/min, well over 10000). Documents and issues return 150KB+ per response, naturally throttling autocannon to ~130-243 req/s, which stays under the limit. Mitigation attempted: --rate flag to cap request rate, but the flag behaved inconsistently across endpoints.
methodology_caveats.rate_limit_finding: The rate limit itself is a SECURITY POSITIVE — it correctly prevents DoS via flooding. Discovered during U4 baseline measurement. Audit-wise this confirms good defense-in-depth, not a defect. Documented in ORIENTATION.md and AUDIT.md.
methodology_caveats.incomplete_data: Programs c=25/50, Weeks c=10/25, and Dashboard at all 3 levels were rate-limited and do not contain meaningful latency data. Listed as 'rate_limited' in results below.

documents.interpretation: Returns ALL 257 documents WITH full content (TipTap JSON) and yjs_state (BYTEA). No projection, no pagination.

issues.interpretation: Issue list returns issue documents. Lighter payload than documents but still substantial.

weeks.interpretation: Returns week documents per program (~35 total weeks expected from seed).

dashboard.interpretation: Returns aggregated dashboard data. Small payload, but rate limit prevented measurement at all concurrency levels.

headline_findings.finding: Documents endpoint shows clear degradation under concurrent load
headline_findings.root_cause_hypothesis: Returns ALL 257 documents WITH full content (TipTap JSON, ~5KB+ per doc) AND yjs_state (BYTEA, ~1-10KB per doc). No column projection, no pagination. Each request loads ~150KB and serializes/transmits it. At higher concurrency, multiple requests compete for the same heavy result set.
headline_findings.u13_target: Documents P95 at c=25 from 283ms → 226ms (-20%) is the PDF improvement target. Achievable by: (a) SELECT only needed columns (id, document_type, title, properties, timestamps), excluding content and yjs_state from list responses; (b) optional: pagination.

headline_findings.finding: Issues endpoint shows similar degradation pattern
headline_findings.root_cause_hypothesis: Same underlying pattern as documents — likely same code path returning full document rows for issue documents. Issues filter by document_type='issue' but otherwise share the documents table.
headline_findings.u13_target: Issues P95 at c=25 from 192ms → 154ms (-20%) — second easy win, same fix as documents.

headline_findings.finding: Small-payload endpoints (programs, dashboard, weeks) hit rate limit
headline_findings.evidence: At max throughput, autocannon sends 5000-7000 req/s on small-response endpoints, exceeding even E2E_TEST mode's 10000/min limit.
headline_findings.interpretation: This is GOOD security posture — Ship has rate limiting that correctly prevents flooding. The rate limit defends against DoS but limits our ability to benchmark sustained max-throughput on lightweight endpoints. Where data is available, programs c=10 (P95=26ms) and weeks c=50 (P95=131ms) show these endpoints are also affected by concurrency but the absolute numbers are much smaller.

improvement_target.pdf_requirement: 20% reduction in P95 response time on at least 2 endpoints, measured under identical load conditions
approach_for_u13: Apply column projection: list-view queries return id, document_type, title, properties, timestamps, ticket_number, parent_id, workspace_id — excluding content (JSONB) and yjs_state (BYTEA).
Add response field projection at API serialization layer if needed (different concerns can need different columns).
Verify via re-running autocannon: documents-c25 P95 should drop from 283ms toward <226ms.
No N+1 detected yet (queries are single-row from a single table); EXPLAIN ANALYZE planned for U4 next step to confirm.
improvement_target.rationale: Response size is the dominant cost. Cutting payload size by excluding content/yjs_state should yield far more than 20% improvement on P95.

additional_protections: Login endpoint has stricter brute-force protection: 5 failed attempts / 15 min (skipSuccessfulRequests=true)
DDoS server-level timeouts in api/src/index.ts: 60s request, 65s keepalive, 66s headers — Slowloris protection
rate_limit_finding_for_audit.audit_classification: Strong defense — calls out as one of the 3 strongest decisions in ORIENTATION.md §3.1

next_step_recommendation: EXPLAIN ANALYZE the documents and issues list queries to confirm the response-size hypothesis and identify any other query-level issues (N+1, missing indexes). That feeds U4's Database Query Efficiency category.
